string.text: Standard 25-round magazine used by the FAMAS F1. The Famas F1 does not use a STANAG compatible system, creating logistic issues for France when working with other nations, leading to the FAMAS G2 adopting the STANAG system.

Modern PMAG-style magazine holding 30 rounds, modified for use with the FAMAS F1. Considerably lighter than its steel counterpart, the ergonomic design facilitates quick loading of cartridges.\n It seems the modifications to make it fit a FAMAS F1 magwell were such that it cannot be reverted.
Extended 40-round polymer STANAG magazine, modified for use with the FAMAS F1.\n It seems the modifications to make it fit a FAMAS F1 magwell were such that it cannot be reverted.

60-round quad-stack STANAG magazine, modified for use with the FAMAS F1. Offers a lot of firepower, but is extremely heavy and the quad-stack nature makes it difficult to load in rounds quickly.\n It seems the modifications to make it fit a FAMAS F1 magwell were such that it cannot be reverted.
